content: According to 1 Thessalonians 1:1, who joined Paul as co-authors of this letter?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:1 — 'Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, to the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace.'

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 1:3, what three things does Paul remember about the Thessalonians?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:3 — '...remembering before our God and Father your work of faith and labor of love and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.'
options: Work of faith, labor of love, steadfastness of hope

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 1:5, the gospel came not only in word but also in what?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:5 — '...our gospel came to you not only in word, but also in power and in the Holy Spirit and with full conviction.'
options: In power, the Holy Spirit, and full conviction

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 1:9, from what did the Thessalonians turn, and to what?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:9 — '...how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God...'
options: From the law to the gospel
From dissolute life to moral living
From idols to serve the living and true God

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 1:10, for whom do believers wait from heaven?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:10 — '...to wait for his Son from heaven, whom he raised from the dead, Jesus who delivers us from the wrath to come.'
options: His Son from heaven, Jesus who delivers from the wrath to come

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 2:7, like whom did Paul and his companions act among the Thessalonians?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:7 — '...we were gentle among you, like a nursing mother taking care of her own children.'
options: Like a nursing mother caring for her own children

content: In 1 Thessalonians 2:11, to whom else does Paul compare himself?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 2:11 — '...we exhorted each one of you as a father with his children.'
options: Like an elder brother to a younger
Like a master to a servant
Like a father with his children

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:3, what is the will of God?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:3 — 'For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you abstain from sexual immorality.'
options: That you go on a mission
Your sanctification — abstain from sexual immorality

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:11, what aspiration must believers have?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:11 — '...aspire to live quietly, and to mind your own affairs, and to work with your hands, as we instructed you.'
options: Live quietly, mind your own affairs, work with your hands

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:16, when the Lord descends from heaven, what accompanies Him?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:16 — 'For the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God.'
options: A cry of command, voice of an archangel, trumpet of God

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:17, what will the living who remain do?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:17 — '...we who are alive, who are left, will be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air, and so we will always be with the Lord.'
options: Stand still on earth awaiting His return
Be caught up together in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air
Withdraw to the wilderness to pray
tags: Caught up to meet the Lord

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:2, how does the day of the Lord come?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:2 — 'For you yourselves are fully aware that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.'
options: Like a thief in the night
tags: Day of Lord like a thief

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:5, what are believers?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:5 — 'For you are all children of light, children of the day. We are not of the night or of the darkness.'
options: Children of light and children of the day

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:8, what breastplate and helmet must believers wear?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:8 — '...having put on the breastplate of faith and love, and for a helmet the hope of salvation.'
options: Breastplate of righteousness, helmet of victory
Breastplate of faith and love; helmet of the hope of salvation
Breastplate of the Word, helmet of the Spirit
Breastplate of wisdom, helmet of grace

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:9, for what has God destined believers?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:9 — 'For God has not destined us for wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ.'
options: To obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18, what is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18 — 'Rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.'
options: Rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:23, what does God preserve blameless?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:23 — 'Now may the God of peace himself sanctify you completely, and may your whole spirit and soul and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.'
options: Whole spirit and soul and body, blameless

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:14, what about those who have fallen asleep in Christ?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:14 — 'For since we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so, through Jesus, God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep.'
options: They will remain in their tombs
God will bring them with Jesus
They will be revived in their old land

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:13, believers do not grieve like whom?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:13 — '...that you may not grieve as others do who have no hope.'
options: Like the greedy of this world
Like others who have no hope
Like those who oppose the faith

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 3:12, what does Paul pray that the Lord increase and abound in believers?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 3:12 — 'and may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all, as we do for you.'
options: Love for one another and for all

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:21-22, after testing everything and holding to what is good, what must believers abstain from?
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:21-22 — 'Test everything; hold fast what is good. Abstain from every form of evil.'
tags: Abstain from every form of evil

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 1:9-10, what did the Thessalonians do? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 1:9-10 — '...you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God, and to wait for his Son from heaven...' Three actions; 'returned to old practices' contradicts.
options: Serve the living and true God
Wait for His Son from heaven

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 4:16, what accompanies the Lord's coming? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 4:16 — '...the Lord himself will descend from heaven with a cry of command, with the voice of an archangel, and with the sound of the trumpet of God.' Three sounds; 'silently unnoticed' is the opposite.
options: The sound of the trumpet of God

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:14, how must believers treat various groups of people? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:14 — '...admonish the idle, encourage the fainthearted, help the weak, be patient with them all.' Three specific groups; 'cursing the apostate' contradicts the spirit of the passage.

content: According to 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18, what three things make up God's will for believers? (select all that apply)
explanation: 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18 — 'Rejoice always, pray without ceasing, give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.' Three commands; 'sleep early' is not in the text.
